title: commiphora confusa
intro: Commiphora confusa, also known as the African myrrh, is a species of plant in the family Burseraceae that is native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Commiphora confusa include the ability to aid in wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi. The herbal preparations of Commiphora confusa include essential oils, extracts, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
